Title: Insights into the missing apiosylation step in flavonoid apiosides biosynthesis of Leguminosae plants

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies UDP-apiose donor required for GuApiGT-catalyzed apiosylation and enabled flavonoid apioside production in enzyme assays and tobacco transient expression.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves in transiently expressed tobacco system
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive flavonoid glycosides; plant growth regulation-like roles; medicinal activity for some members such as liquiritin apioside
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coupled enzyme assay; Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression; LC/MS metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Arabidopsis thaliana UAXS was introduced into the in vitro assay to generate UDP-apiose from UDP-GlcA. 2. Co-expression of UAXS with GuApiGT in Nicotiana benthamiana enabled detectable production of isoliquiritin apioside after precursor feeding. 3. UAXS was included in the engineered UDP-donor module for de novo biosynthesis of flavonoid apiosides in tobacco.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA945816 (used for G. uralensis transcriptome analysis, not specifically for UAXS)
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This gene is an upstream donor-supply enzyme rather than the main core discovery target of the paper; included because it was experimentally required for the studied metabolic reconstruction.
Common_Genes.Promoters: 35S promoter in tobacco transient expression context
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression; precursor feeding with UDP-GlcA and/or flavonoid intermediates
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful donor-supply module component for engineering apioside biosynthesis platforms in plants or heterologous systems.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Served as a key upstream flavonoid-pathway bait gene for co-expression mining of GuApiGT and was used in tobacco engineering module for flavonoid aglycone supply.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots of G. uralensis; engineered tobacco leaves
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression analysis; pathway reconstruction in tobacco
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GuCHS was used as a bait gene together with GuCHR to identify candidate apiosyltransferase genes from G. uralensis transcriptomes. 2. Its expression pattern correlated with flavonoid apioside accumulation in roots. 3. It was part of the engineered aglycone module for de novo apioside production.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Background/upstream pathway gene, not the main functional target of the study.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Higher in roots than cortex/leaves in context of co-expression analysis
Common_Genes.Promoters: 35S promoter in tobacco transient expression context
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tissue comparison among roots, cortexes, leaves
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream module gene for reconstructing flavonoid apioside biosynthesis.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Served as a key upstream bait gene for co-expression discovery of GuApiGT and was used in the engineered aglycone module for isoliquiritigenin production.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots of G. uralensis; engineered tobacco leaves
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression analysis; pathway reconstruction in tobacco
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GuCHR was used with GuCHS as co-expression bait to identify GuApiGT. 2. Its expression was associated with root-enriched flavonoid apioside biosynthesis. 3. It was incorporated in tobacco module 1 to synthesize isoliquiritigenin.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Background/upstream pathway gene, not the central discovery target.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Higher in roots than cortex/leaves in context of co-expression analysis
Common_Genes.Promoters: 35S promoter in tobacco transient expression context
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tissue comparison among roots, cortexes, leaves
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful precursor-supply gene for metabolic engineering of licorice-type flavonoids.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UDP-dependent glycosyltransferase (UGT); GT-B fold; PSPG box with unique 45-aa motif RLGSDH
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers apiose from UDP-apiose to the 2''-OH of glycosyl moieties on flavonoid and other phenolic glycosides, especially flavonoid 7- or 4'-O-glycosides, generating 2''-O-apiosides.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Flavonoid glycosides such as liquiritin and isoliquiritin; UDP-apiose as sugar donor
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Liquiritin apioside; Isoliquiritin apioside; diverse 2''-O-apiosylated flavonoid/phenolic glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Flavonoid apiosides including liquiritin apioside and isoliquiritin apioside
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots of Glycyrrhiza uralensis; engineered Nicotiana benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive natural products; some flavonoid apiosides are believed to have hormone-like roles in plant growth regulation; liquiritin apioside has antitussive activity
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant for the missing apiosylation step; key missing enzyme identified in pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Efficiently and regio-selectively produces 2''-O-apiosylated flavonoid glycosides and enables de novo biosynthesis of multiple flavonoid apiosides in tobacco.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression-guided cloning; recombinant enzyme assay; LC/MS and NMR product identification; kinetic analysis; crystal structure; mutagenesis; MD/QM-MM calculations; transient metabolic engineering in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GuApiGT from Glycyrrhiza uralensis was identified as the missing phenolic apiosyltransferase that converts liquiritin and isoliquiritin into their 2''-O-apiosides. 2. It shows strict sugar donor selectivity for UDP-apiose and broad acceptor promiscuity toward many flavonoid, lignan, and coumarin glycosides. 3. Structural and mutagenesis analyses revealed that the unique 45-aa PSPG box and RLGSDH motif underlie UDP-apiose selectivity. 4. Introducing GuApiGT with upstream genes into Nicotiana benthamiana enabled de novo production of at least 10 flavonoid apiosides, with liquiritin apioside and isoliquiritin apioside reaching 5.46 and 4.73 mg/g DW.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Co-expressed with GuCHS and GuCHR; enriched in roots relative to cortex/leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Origin_Species: Higher in roots than cortexes and leaves
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: UAXS (functional coupling in donor supply system)
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tissue-dependent expression; transient expression in tobacco; precursor feeding; sugar donor availability
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: 35S promoter in Nicotiana benthamiana transient expression
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: GuGT14 replacement was needed in pathway reconstruction due to byproduct formation from poor regioselectivity of glycosylation module, but no direct growth penalty for GuApiGT was reported
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: G. uralensis RNA-seq transcriptomes: NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA945816; PDB: 8HZZ
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GuApiGT contains an unusual 45-aa PSPG box rather than the canonical 44-aa box; active-site motif R368-L369-G370-S371-D372-H373 is critical for donor selectivity. Mutagenesis converted donor preference toward UDP-Xyl or UDP-Glc.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value enzyme for metabolic engineering of bioactive flavonoid apiosides; suitable for pathway reconstruction and gene stacking with donor-supply and upstream flavonoid genes; structural motif enables protein engineering to redesign sugar donor usage.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UDP-dependent glycosyltransferase with 45-aa PSPG box and conserved RLGSDH-type motif
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes apiosyl transfer to flavonoid glycosides in vitro, validating it as an apiosyltransferase from Leguminosae.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Validated as a Leguminosae apiosyltransferase supporting generality of the GuApiGT-like motif.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning; enzyme catalysis assay; LC/MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PtApiGT was identified from the Leguminosae candidate set using the unique PSPG-box filter. 2. Cloned PtApiGT showed apiosyltransferase activity in enzyme assays. 3. Its characterization supports conservation of the GuApiGT-like apiosylation mechanism in Leguminosae.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Candidate homolog search from online transcriptome databases; accession OQ230795
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of four additional experimentally verified Leguminosae ApiGTs.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative apiosyltransferase resource for pathway engineering in legumes or heterologous systems.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UDP-dependent glycosyltransferase with 45-aa PSPG box and conserved RLGSDH-type motif
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes apiosyl transfer to flavonoid glycosides in vitro, validating it as an apiosyltransferase from Leguminosae.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Validated as a Leguminosae apiosyltransferase supporting motif generality.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene synthesis/cloning; enzyme catalysis assay; LC/MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SsApiGT was among the candidate ApiGTs found in Leguminosae. 2. It was experimentally shown to possess apiosyltransferase activity. 3. Its sequence is highly conserved with GuApiGT.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Candidate homolog search from online transcriptome databases; accession OQ230794
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Full length gene was synthesized for testing.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative enzyme source for apioside biosynthetic engineering.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UDP-dependent glycosyltransferase with 45-aa PSPG box and conserved RLGSDH-type motif
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes apiosyl transfer to flavonoid glycosides in vitro.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Validated as a GuApiGT-like apiosyltransferase from another Glycyrrhiza species.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GgApiGT was cloned from Glycyrrhiza glabra. 2. It showed apiosyltransferase activity in vitro. 3. Its validation supports conservation of apiosyltransferases in flavonoid-apioside-producing Leguminosae.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Candidate homolog search from online transcriptome databases; accession OQ230797
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative Glycyrrhiza apiosyltransferase for synthetic biology.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UDP-dependent glycosyltransferase with 45-aa PSPG box and conserved RLGSDH-type motif
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes apiosyl transfer to flavonoid glycosides in vitro.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GiApiGT was cloned from Glycyrrhiza inflata. 2. It showed apiosyltransferase activity in vitro. 3. This further supports the widespread occurrence of GuApiGT-like enzymes in Leguminosae.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Candidate homolog search from online transcriptome databases; accession OQ230796
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative enzyme source for apioside production systems.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Flavonoid glycoside biosynthesis upstream of apiosylation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Regio-selectively and efficiently catalyzes 4'/4-O-glycosylation of liquiritigenin and isoliquiritigenin to produce liquiritin and isoliquiritin, respectively.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Improved de novo biosynthesis of isoliquiritin apioside in tobacco by supplying regioselective glycosylated precursors and reducing byproduct formation compared with GuGT14.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression selection; recombinant enzyme assay; pathway reconstruction in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC/MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GuGT53 was discovered from G. uralensis based on an expression pattern similar to GuCHS, GuCHR, and GuApiGT. 2. It efficiently and regio-selectively forms liquiritin and isoliquiritin from their aglycones. 3. Replacing GuGT14 with GuGT53 improved isoliquiritin apioside production in tobacco by minimizing a major byproduct.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Similar expression pattern to GuCHS, GuCHR, and GuApiGT
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: 35S promoter in tobacco transient expression context
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Using the alternative enzyme GuGT14 caused poor regioselectivity and byproduct 7a formation; GuGT53 alleviated this issue
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although not the primary discovery target, it became a key enabling upstream enzyme in the tobacco reconstruction module.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for precise precursor glycosylation in engineered production of licorice flavonoid apiosides.
